title: Mass. man pleads guilty to plotting to assassinate Trump Cabinet member
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ryan Michael English, 24, from South Deerfield, pleaded guilty to plotting an attack on Treasury Secretary nominee Scott Bessent.
English was charged with carrying a knife and Molotov cocktails on U.S. Capitol grounds before turning himself in.
English faces a maximum of 15 years in prison and a $260,000 fine under the plea agreement signed last week.

title: Salisbury woman found guilty of murdering boyfriend with antifreeze
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was found guilty of first-degree murder for killing Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in Salisbury in 2022.
Prosecutors stated Church poisoned Fowler due to jealousy over his affair, with evidence including a suspicious Powerade bottle.
Church's sentencing is set for Thursday morning, where she is expected to receive life in prison.

title: New England nominees for James Beard Awards 2026: See chefs, restaurants who made the cut
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: No Massachusetts restaurants or chefs are nominees for the 2026 James Beard Awards from New England.
The Shipwright's Daughter in Mystic, Connecticut, is nominated for Outstanding Chef among other categories.
Winners will be announced at the annual James Beard gala in Chicago on June 15, 2026.

title: Dorchester woman wins $2 million on scratch ticket bought at local liquor store
author: John Waller
bullets: Dorchester's Tuyet Le won $2 million from a scratch ticket purchased at Savin Hill Wine & Spirits, Dorchester Ave.
Le opted for a cash payout, receiving a $1.3 million lump sum before taxes, and plans to purchase a home.
The liquor store that sold the winning ticket received a $20,000 bonus from Massachusetts State Lottery.

title: She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Caylie McMahon, once told she might never walk again, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation.
McMahon underwent spinal surgery in 2021 and recovered at Spaulding, defying expectations and returning to field hockey.
Running with her friend Lindsey Stagg, McMahon aims to support Spaulding Rehabilitation's Race for Rehab team.

title: Patriots opt for line help in latest NFL mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are projected to select Florida's Caleb Banks with the 31st overall pick in the NFL Draft.
Caleb Banks, a 6-foot-6, 327-pound defensive tackle, impressed scouts despite a previous foot injury.
Patriots' mock draft includes picks like Max Iheanachor, Sam Roush, and Ja’Kobi Lane in later rounds.

title: Retired Scituate couple mourned after fatal helicopter crash
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, died in a helicopter crash near Kalalau Beach, Kauai.
A Hughes OH-6 Cayuse helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation crashed with five people on board, killing three.
Federal authorities are investigating the crash that also claimed the life of a 40-year-old woman on the helicopter.

title: Mass. school bus driver fired after allegedly urinating inside bus with students onboard
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 71-year-old school bus driver in Swansea was fired for allegedly urinating inside a bus with students onboard.
The incident occurred while transporting 12 pre-K to second-grade students to Hoyle Elementary School on Monday morning.
No criminal charges have been filed; Swansea Police and school officials are investigating the incident.

title: Everything you need to know about Zach Bryan’s planned Jack Kerouac Center in Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Zach Bryan purchased Lowell's St. Jean Baptiste Church to create the Jack Kerouac Center, celebrating Kerouac's legacy.
The planned center will include a museum, performance venue, recording studio, and restaurant, accomodating up to 1,200 attendees.
Renovation costs for the historic church are estimated at $25-30 million, with fundraising efforts underway for project completion.

title: Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Elena Castellini from Brookline is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
Castellini is raising money for One World Strong Foundation, founded by a 2013 bombing survivor.
The foundation supports trauma survivors worldwide, including projects in Ukraine.
